title: Information technology directors
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as electronic data processing, information systems, systems analysis, and computer programming. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. Design and implement computer and information networks, such as local area networks (LAN), wide area networks (WAN), intranets, extranets, and other data communications networks. Perform network modeling, analysis, and planning, including analysis of capacity needs for network infrastructures. May also design network and computer security measures. May research and recommend network and data communications hardware and software. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Manage backup, security, and user help systems, and implement controls to provide security for operating systems, software, and data.
Meet with department heads, managers, supervisors, vendors, and others to solicit cooperation, resolve problems, and determine customer needs.
Provide users with technical support for computer problems, diagnose network and device problems, and document user support activity including system problems and resolutions.
Consult with users, management, vendors, and technicians to assess computing needs, system requirements, and evaluate data processing proposals to assess project feasibility and requirements.
Purchase necessary equipment and prepare purchase requisitions for computer hardware, software, and telecommunications equipment.
Review project plans and requests to plan and coordinate project activity, preparing operational reports and monitoring project schedules and budgets.
Recruit, hire, train, and supervise staff, developing and interpreting organizational goals, policies, and procedures.
Control operational budget and expenditures, and estimate costs and time for system or component implementation and operations.
Review and approve all system charts and programs prior to their implementation.
Develop computer information resources, including data security and control, disaster recovery plans, and recommend improvements in technology use and needs.
Stay abreast of advances in technology and keep up with changes in industry practices and emerging telecommunications technology through continuous education and engagement.

Assign and review the work of systems analysts, programmers, and other computer-related workers, and direct the daily operations of the department including analyzing workflow and establishing priorities.
Monitor and analyze network performance and system performance, and perform capacity planning.
Document technical specifications and operating standards for telecommunications equipment, procedures for hardware and software installation, and develop network-related documentation.
Install, maintain, or coordinate installation of new or modified hardware, software, or programming modules for telecommunications systems and ensure proper user account management and access.
Communicate with telecommunications vendors to obtain pricing and technical specifications for available hardware, software, or services, and maintain inventory of telecommunications equipment.
Assess existing facilities' needs for new or modified telecommunications systems, and design, implement, and evaluate network security measures.
Instruct in the use of voice, video, and data communications systems, and participate in network technology upgrade projects.
Prepare detailed network specifications, including diagrams and recommended technologies, and develop conceptual or physical network designs.
Perform preventive maintenance, backup, and recovery procedures on networks, and implement solutions for network problems.
Coordinate network operations, maintenance, repairs, or upgrades, including the installation of new equipment and evaluating requests for system modifications.
Design and organize product awareness sessions, and prepare design presentations and proposals for staff or customers including documenting plans for project delivery.
Conduct analyses related to geospatial data building and develop data models and integration methods for different products to ensure proper functionality.
Identify industry trends in database and geographic information systems for advising management, and evaluate new or modified software or testing programs.
Supervise maintenance activities of telecommunications equipment and develop procedures to report on network availability, reliability, and capacity.
Develop and maintain project reporting systems alongside establishing optimum parameters for databases according to user needs and compliance requirements.
Investigate customer problems referred by technical support, and provide feedback and recommendations on software usability and functionality based on diagnostic results.
Document all processes related to software testing and develop automated tools for testing while monitoring resolutions and compliance with standards.
Train users and provide clear, detailed descriptions of software specifications to ensure understanding of system capabilities and compliance with technical standards.
Evaluate software functionalities, performance and compatibility through testing scenarios while communicating insights or recommendations for potential improvements.
Participate in product design reviews to ensure all functional requirements are met and document plans for systems accordingly.
Develop or document style guidelines for Web site content.
Test programs or databases, correct errors, and make necessary modifications.
Work as part of a project team to coordinate database development and determine project scope and limitations.
Write and code logical and physical database descriptions, and specify identifiers of database to management system or direct others in coding descriptions.
Design or coordinate the development of integrated Geographic Information Systems (GIS) spatial or non-spatial databases.
Provide technical support to users or clients regarding the maintenance, development, or operation of Geographic Information Systems (GIS) databases, equipment, or applications.
Develop training materials for, or make presentations to, Geographic Information Systems (GIS) users.
Assess the quality of security controls, using performance indicators.
Conduct investigations of information security breaches to identify vulnerabilities and evaluate the damage.
Coordinate monitoring of networks or systems for security breaches or intrusions.
Develop response and recovery strategies for security breaches.
Develop or install software, such as firewalls and data encryption programs, to protect sensitive information.
Provide technical support to computer users for installation and use of security products.
Integrate new procedures or requirements for data collection, processing, or reporting.
Test new software packages for use in Web operations or other applications.
Create, analyze, report, convert, or transfer data, using specialized applications program software.
Develop and document database architectures and architectural strategies at the modeling, design and implementation stages to address business or industry requirements.
Collaborate to understand business or industry requirements and design applications that support them.
Document installation or configuration procedures to allow maintenance and repetition.
Develop or maintain archived procedures, procedural codes, or queries for applications.
Develop or verify methods to ensure database developments adhere to industry standards.
Identify or address interoperability requirements.
Develop and implement data extraction procedures from other systems, such as administration, billing, or claims.
Research, test, or verify proper functioning of software patches and fixes.
Establish functional or system standards to address operational requirements, quality requirements, and design constraints.
Design and conduct hardware or software tests.
Develop system engineering, software engineering, system integration, or distributed system architectures.
Define and analyze objectives, scope, issues, or organizational impact of information systems.

Perform ongoing hardware and software maintenance operations, including installing or upgrading hardware or software.

Direct the analysis, development, and operation of complete computer systems.

Provide customers or installation teams guidelines for implementing secure systems.
Identify system data, hardware, or software components required to meet user needs.
Provide technical guidance or support for the development or troubleshooting of systems.
Evaluate current or emerging technologies to consider factors such as cost, portability, compatibility, or usability.
Collaborate with engineers or software developers to select appropriate design solutions or ensure the compatibility of system components.
Verify stability, interoperability, portability, security, or scalability of system architecture.
Document design specifications, installation instructions, and other system-related information.
Provide advice on project costs, design concepts, or design changes.
Communicate with staff or clients to understand specific system requirements.
Evaluate existing systems to determine effectiveness, and suggest changes to meet organizational requirements.
